architecture: Pi coding agent extension that intercepts tool_call/tool_result events, matches commands against a filter registry, applies output compression to reduce token usage, tracks savings in SQLite, and exposes /rtk command with gain/discover/settings subcommands. Closure-based state pattern with config resolution from env vars > settings.json > defaults.
trustBoundaries: tool_call event: external input from Pi agent — toolName, input.command, input.path, toolCallId stored in commandMap
tool_result event: external input from Pi agent — content[].text (raw command output), isError flag, toolCallId used for Map lookup
settings.json (global ~/.pi/agent/settings.json and project .pi/settings.json): JSON.parse of user-controlled files, values flow into config
tee file I/O: raw command output written to disk at ~/.pi/agent/rtk/tee/ — fs.writeFileSync with user-influenced content and command-derived filenames
SQLite operations: command strings, char counts, filter names, session IDs written to DB — potential injection via command strings
Environment variables: RTK_* env vars parsed as bools/ints, flow into config
filter.apply(): each filter receives baseCommand + cleanText (external origin), regex/parsing on untrusted output
